Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search the active relocations board to identify the records for 'Raj Patel' and 'Marcus Webb'. Tool: airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records. Parameters: "Raj Patel", "Marcus Webb".
OE 2: Search internal communications to discover that the April 10 Raj Patel move failed due to a security breach, while the April 21 move is valid. Tool: conversations_search_messages or search_emails. Parameters: "Raj Patel", "breach", "SentinelChain".
OE 3: Update the Airtable record for the breached April 10 Raj Patel move, changing its status to 'Cancelled'. Tool: airtable_update_records. Parameters: record ID, Status: "Cancelled".
OE 4: Search communications or CRM to verify that the two 'Marcus Webb' records belong to distinct employees at different companies (BrightLoop and Canopy Health) and should not be cancelled. Tool: search_emails or crm_search_contacts. Parameters: "Marcus Webb".
OE 5: Post a message in the operations Slack channel announcing that the board confusion has been cleared up. Tool: conversations_add_message. Parameters: channel: "#operations" (C006), text: summary of board cleanup.
OE 6: Search communications to find Mina's escalation regarding the delayed Road Runner vehicle for the BrightLoop client. Tool: conversations_search_messages or search_emails. Parameters: "Marcus Webb", "Mina", "vehicle".
OE 7: Search client emails to count exactly 2 unanswered follow-up emails from Marcus Webb asking for a vehicle update. Tool: search_emails. Parameters: "Marcus Webb", "car delivery status".
OE 8: Search incident reports or communications to discover Blessing's operational failure: she countersigned the packing checklist remotely without physically verifying the foam density. Tool: conversations_search_messages or search_emails. Parameters: "Mosaic", "Blessing", "packing", "foam".
OE 9: Search vendor documentation or communications to find that MoveOps' vendor liability coverage limit is $50,000 per item (applicable to the Heartland-transported Mosaic prototype). Tool: search_emails or conversations_search_messages. Parameters: "Heartland", "liability", "coverage".
OE 10: Create a new tracking ticket documenting Blessing's remote sign-off failure and the $50,000 liability limit, and assign it to Marcus Thorne. Tool: linear_create_issue. Parameters: title/description containing the failure and "$50,000", assignee: Marcus Thorne.
OE 11: Search tracking tickets to find the existing issue regarding Keiko Tanaka's missing DOT certificate and discover its ID (system ID: issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic, display key: OPS-2). Tool: linear_list_issues. Parameters: query: "Keiko Tanaka" or "DOT".
OE 12: Search vendor emails to find Greg Pallone's confirmation that Swift does possess the signed DOT placarding certificate. Tool: search_emails. Parameters: "Swift", "Keiko Tanaka", "certificate".
OE 13: Update the existing tracking ticket (system ID: issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic / display key: OPS-2) with the confirmation that Swift has the certificate. Tool: linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment. Parameters: id: "issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic", comments/status update.
OE 14: Search vendor emails to find the exact DOT documentation surcharge amount of $500 announced by Swift earlier in the month. Tool: search_emails. Parameters: "Swift", "surcharge", "DOT".
OE 15: Lookup the email addresses for Marcus Thorne and Chloe Vance to use in the final email. Tool: contacts_search_contacts. Parameters: "Marcus Thorne", "Chloe Vance".
OE 16: Send a summary email to Marcus Thorne, CCing Chloe Vance, detailing the Patel/Webb billing verdict, the exact count of 2 ignored vehicle emails, the title of the newly created Mosaic Linear ticket, and the $500 Swift surcharge. Tool: send_email. Parameters: to: marcus.thorne@moveops.com, cc: chloe.vance@moveops.com, body containing all requested summaries.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The status of the Raj Patel record corresponding to the failed April 10 move was updated to 'Cancelled' (via airtable_update_records). (Exact Match)
outcome
 ✗✓✓✗✓✓ 67%
✗Run #1The agent cancelled 'recRajPatelChicagoDenver' (the April 21-23 planned move, Blessing). The criteria require cancelling the April 10 move ('recRajPatelAuditNote0411'), which failed due to a security breach (movers loaded without IT escort). The agent cancelled the wrong record.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows airtable_update_records was called with id 'recRajPatelAuditNote0411' and Status set to 'Cancelled'. The tool result confirms the update was applied to that record.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call with record ID 'recRajPatelAuditNote0411' and Status set to 'Cancelled'. The result confirms this record has Move Start Date '2026-04-10', correctly identifying and cancelling the April 10 breached move record.
✗Run #4The agent cancelled record 'recRajPatelChicagoDenver', which it identified as the April 21-23 planning record under Blessing Okafor. The criterion requires cancelling the April 10 move record (which failed due to a security breach). The agent kept the April 10 record and cancelled the April 21-23 record — the opposite of what was required.
✓Run #5The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call with record id 'recRajPatelAuditNote0411' and Status field set to 'Cancelled'. This correctly targets the April 10 breach record.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows an airtable_update_records call with base_id 'appMoveOpsOps001', table_id 'tblRelocations01', record id 'recRajPatelAuditNote0411', and Status set to 'Cancelled'. The tool result confirms the update was applied to the April 10 move record.
2 The status of the legitimate Raj Patel record corresponding to the April 21-23 move was NOT cancelled or altered in Airtable.
outcome
 ✗✓✓✗✓✓ 67%
✗Run #1The agent cancelled 'recRajPatelChicagoDenver', which is the April 21-23 record. Per the criteria, this is the valid, ongoing relocation for NorthWind Technologies that must not be modified. The agent incorrectly identified it as stale and cancelled it.
✓Run #2The agent only called airtable_update_records once, targeting recRajPatelAuditNote0411. The record recRajPatelChicagoDenver (April 21-23 move) was never passed to any update or delete call.
✓Run #3Only one airtable_update_records call was made for Raj Patel, targeting the April 10 breached record ('recRajPatelAuditNote0411'). There is no update call targeting the April 21 re-planned record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver), confirming it was left intact.
✗Run #4The agent cancelled record 'recRajPatelChicagoDenver', which it itself described as the April 21-23 move record (the stale planning record). The criterion explicitly states the April 21-23 record is the valid, ongoing relocation and must NOT be modified. The agent cancelled exactly this record.
✓Run #5The airtable_update_records call only updated 'recRajPatelAuditNote0411'. The record 'recRajPatelChicagoDenver' (April 21-23) was never passed to any update or delete call.
✓Run #6The only airtable_update_records call in the trajectory targets 'recRajPatelAuditNote0411' (the April 10 breached record). There is no update or delete call for the April 21-23 Raj Patel record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver). The #operations post and email also confirm this record was left as In Progress.

6 The title or description of the newly created Linear ticket mentions that Blessing's operational failure was approving the packing remotely without physical verification (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Linear ticket description documents Blessing's failure: she 'signed off on Heartland Movers packing checklist remotely on April 14... did not visually verify the anti-vibration foam density... accepted verbal confirmation only from Heartland crew lead.' This is a clear description of remote approval without physical verification.
✓Run #2The linear_create_issue description explicitly states Blessing 'countersigned Heartland's packing checklist remotely via digital signature... while she was physically located in Philadelphia' and 'did not visually inspect the crating materials,' directly documenting the remote approval without physical verification failure.
✓Run #3The Linear ticket description explicitly documents: 'Blessing Okafor signed off on Heartland's packing checklist remotely on April 14... did not visually verify the anti-vibration foam density against the 4-inch minimum specification... relied solely on verbal confirmation from Heartland crew lead Marcus Delaney.' This matches the required operational failure documentation.
✓Run #4The Linear ticket description explicitly states: 'She approved Heartland's packing checklist remotely via digital signature based solely on a verbal phone call with Heartland crew lead...without physically inspecting the anti-vibration foam crating.' This clearly documents Blessing's remote approval without physical verification.

✓Run #6The linear_create_issue description explicitly states: 'She remotely countersigned Heartland's packing checklist via digital signature...She did NOT visually inspect the crating materials or verify that the anti-vibration foam met the 4-inch minimum density specification.' outcome

9 The update to the Keiko Tanaka DOT ticket stated that Swift Relocations confirmed they possess the signed DOT placarding certificate (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✗✓✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The ticket update states 'Greg Pallone (Swift Operations Manager) confirmed via email on ~April 20 that the signed DOT hazmat placarding certificate exists. It was signed by driver Mike Becerra at Columbus pickup on April 15, 2026.' This confirms Swift's possession of the certificate.
✓Run #2The linear_update_issue description states Greg Pallone confirmed that 'DOT hazmat placarding WAS completed at time of transport by Swift driver Mike Becerra' and that 'the Mosaic Equipment Handling Guidelines liability waiver was also signed by Mike Becerra,' confirming Swift possesses the signed certificate.
✗Run #3While the update content does confirm Swift's possession of the certificate, it was placed on the wrong ticket (OPS-5 instead of OPS-2). Since the update was not applied to the correct 'Keiko Tanaka DOT ticket' (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic / OPS-2), this criterion is not satisfied.
✓Run #4The linear_update_issue call includes: 'Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations confirmed via email that the DOT hazmat placarding WAS completed at time of transport.' This clearly states Swift confirmed they possess the signed certificate.
✓Run #5The updated ticket states Greg Pallone at Swift confirmed the placarding was completed, 'The certificate was signed by Mike at time of loading and countersigned by the receiving party at Swift's Seattle storage facility', confirming Swift possesses the signed certificate.
✓Run #6The linear_update_issue description states: 'Greg Pallone at Swift confirmed via email...that driver Mike Becerra completed DOT hazmat placarding...The signed placarding certificate and Mosaic laser equipment liability waiver are both on file in Swift's compliance archive (job #SR-2026-04-1538).' This confirms Swift possesses the signed certificate, though they have not yet sent it.
